paper_title: Geochemical Analysis of Lake Moossee Sediments

methods.description: This method involves the use of X-ray fluorescence (XRF) scanning to analyze sediment cores and subsamples for geochemical composition, followed by preparation of these subsamples for further analysis.

steps.description: Scanning X-ray fluorescence (XRF) was performed at continuous 1 mm steps with the Avaatech core scanner at ETH Zurich.
steps.searchable_summary: perform XRF scanning on sediment cores using Avaatech core scanner

steps.description: 43 discrete sediment subsamples (1-cm thick slices) were taken along the core with higher sampling density in sections with large gradients in the composition of XRF and anoxia data.
steps.searchable_summary: extract and prepare sediment subsamples for analysis

steps.description: The outer oxidized layers of the slices were removed and the subsamples were freeze-dried and homogenized.
steps.searchable_summary: remove oxidized layers, freeze-dry, and homogenize sediment subsamples

methods.description: This method describes the sequential extraction of Fe and Mn fractions from sediment samples and their subsequent analysis using ICP-MS.

steps.description: Seven fractions (F1-F7) of Fe and Mn were determined following a modified sequential extraction procedure by Hall et al. (1996).
steps.searchable_summary: perform sequential extraction of Fe and Mn fractions from sediment samples

steps.description: Element concentrations were measured by inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (ICP-MS) with a collision cell in helium mode.
The accuracy of the analysis was checked by digesting the certified reference material San Joaquin Soil (SRM2709a, NIST).
steps.searchable_summary: validate analysis accuracy using certified reference material
